symbols: manger with straw — humble birth of the King of Kings
swaddling clothes — fulfillment of the angelic sign (Luke 2:12)
lamb — Christ as the Lamb of God
praying hands of Mary — contemplative devotion
shepherds in rough clothing — the lowly as first witnesses
description: A Baroque Nativity scene showing the Adoration of the Shepherds. The Infant Jesus lies in a straw manger at the center, wrapped in white swaddling clothes and emanating a soft glow. The Virgin Mary, wearing a dark blue veil, kneels in prayer beside Him. Joseph stands behind with hands joined in prayer. Several shepherds in rough, earthy clothing gather around with expressions of wonder and devotion. A white lamb lies at the bottom of the composition. The scene is set in a humble stable with a landscape visible in the background.
theological_description: The Adoration of the Shepherds from Luke 2:8-20 shows the first witnesses to the Incarnation. God chose shepherds — among the lowest social classes — as the first to receive the angelic announcement and visit the newborn Messiah, establishing a pattern of divine preference for the humble and lowly (1 Corinthians 1:27-28). The lamb in the foreground foreshadows Christ's identity as the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world (John 1:29). Mary's contemplative posture reflects Luke 2:19: she 'treasured up all these things, pondering them in her heart.' The humble stable setting reveals that God enters the world not in royal splendor but in poverty.

scripture_refs.reason: The shepherds visiting the newborn Jesus in the manger
The sign: a baby wrapped in swaddling clothes, lying in a manger
The Lamb of God foreshadowed by the lamb in the scene
Mary pondering these things in her heart, shown in her prayerful posture
